rationale.hitl-workflow: Developers impose checkpoints, bounded delegation, review, and authority over agent changes.
rationale.rules-based-checks: Participants validate agent changes with tests, linters, execution, and systematic testing.
rationale.ai-review: Developers use agents as PR reviewers and second eyes on code.
rationale.quality-debt: Empirical findings report messy, overengineered, poorly integrated, less maintainable agent code.
rationale.agent-scope-drift: Agents exceed requested scope; developers restrict work to small verified chunks.
rationale.oversight-explanation: Developers monitor agent reasoning and inspect outputs to understand behavior.
rationale.survey-input: Survey quantifies developers’ priorities, task preferences, strategies, and agent sentiments.
rationale.counterpoint: Findings oppose autonomous vibe-coding claims: professionals retain supervision and control.
rationale.agentic: Study examines IDE and terminal agents performing multi-step codebase changes.
rationale.steering: Prompting, context provision, planning, and task decomposition substantively shape generation.
